title: Jaltomata bicolor
intro: Jaltomata bicolor, also known as the two-colored jaltomata, is a species of flowering plant in the nightshade family, Solanaceae. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, including its ability to lower blood pressure, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial and antioxidant activities, contributing to its potential health benefits. The bioactive constituents of Jaltomata bicolor include glycoalkaloids, phenolic compounds, and flavonoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Jaltomata bicolor is often used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, including hypertension, fever, and digestive issues.
taxonomy: Jaltomata bicolor is a species of flowering plant in the nightshade family (Solanaceae). It belongs to the genus Jaltomata, which consists of about 70 species of flowering plants. Jaltomata bicolor is native to South America and is a member of the subfamily Solanoideae. The plant is a perennial species that produces small white or yellow flowers, and its classification reflects its close relationship to other nightshade family members. Through its classification, Jaltomata bicolor's evolutionary history and its position within the plant kingdom can be understood.

It is used in traditional medicine to treat a range of ailments, including fever, skin conditions, and respiratory issues.
